title: Puncture Wound (Nails, Animal Bites)
symptoms: Deep, narrow hole in the skin
immediate_steps: Stop the bleeding with gentle pressure
Rinse the wound for 5-10 minutes under running water
Clean the area around the wound with soap
Apply an antibiotic ointment and a bandage
Seek medical attention for deep wounds, animal bites, or if a tetanus shot is needed
avoid: Do not probe or try to clean deep inside a puncture wound
Do not use iodine or hydrogen peroxide
Do not remove large embedded objects (leave them and go to ER)

title: Embedded Object (Knife, Glass, Wood)
symptoms: Object sticking out of the body
immediate_steps: Apply pressure around the edges of the object to control bleeding, not on the object itself
Stabilize the object by packing bulky dressings (like rolled-up gauze or cloths) around it and taping them down
avoid: DO NOT remove the object (it may be acting as a plug to stop severe bleeding)
Do not cut the object shorter unless it is completely unmanageable to transport
